shared_properties: Umbra keeps the 32x48 px silhouette envelope and center-bottom pivot established by the idle base state.
Core palette metadata remains primary #C9A84C, background #0D0B14, and accent #D4682A while flare colors stay inside specs.colors_hex.
Eye-glow position and mist-fringe contour remain recognizable so the state still reads as Umbra.
differences: Five frames add intense white-edge bloom and torso contraction to show direct light contact increasing exposure.
The flare pattern alternates between left and right body edges to stay readable under multiple beam directions.
Amber fracture lines appear only on peak danger frames to signal imminent HP loss.

reference_images.label: gdd_markdown
reference_images.purpose: Grounds exposure buildup, HP loss, and the exposure animation state in the GDD.

reference_images.label: moodboard
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the contrast between safe darkness and hazardous white light.

reference_images.label: umbra_character
reference_images.purpose: Grounds Umbra silhouette so the damage state remains recognizable.

bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load the exposure atlas during FloorLoading and use it while ExposureState indicates active beam contact.
runtime_notes: Pulse frames at 10 fps while exposure is nonzero.
Render the sheet above shadow-safe overlays.
Avoid procedural color shifts beyond the authored atlas frames.

context.usage: Damage feedback state used whenever Umbra overlaps a light-danger region and the exposure meter is climbing.

pose_notes: Peak flare expands 2 px beyond the shoulder line.
Amber fracture lines occupy fewer than 12 visible pixels per frame.
The lowest mist fringe remains anchored to the baseline.

acceptance_criteria: Sprite sheet is exactly 160x48 px with 5 frames of 32x48 px and 0 px spacing.
White-edge bloom reads as beam danger without hiding Umbra silhouette identity.
The state is visually distinct from idle, run, and grab within one glance.
